author: nflaig
content: need more eyes on https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/583, anyone using `kzg_commitments` from the event? (note we will keep emitting them until gloas either way but drop them for slots after gloas)

author: tersec
content: I like it, as before, it's artificial now to have those involved, the CL has to fetch them separately

author: tersec
content: IMO the gloas version is just a separate event

author: nflaig
content: you mean without bumping the event version? the current way the events are defined (bascially using the example data as schema) isn't great, so it's hard to express pre/post fork types, but we kinda did the same with the attestation event too and it didn't cause issues also considering they are just JSON so adding/removing fields across forks shouldn't be a huge deal

author: tersec
content: well, in which case the version should be bumped

author: tersec
content: the overriding principle for me is that these messages are raw-ish transcriptions

author: nflaig
content: there is no version on the events though

author: tersec
content: this one is not anymore

author: tersec
content: so give it a different name

author: tersec
content: informal versioning

author: tersec
content: they should not rely on the client to look up anything from a database etc

author: tersec
content: the fulu version did not but glosa changes how columns work

author: nflaig
content: we discussed this for eletrca regarding the attestation event and the outcome was that we are fine with changing the format instead of adding a attestation_v2 event

author: tersec
content: that works too

author: nflaig
content: let me find the discussion around this, seems to be the same problem we have now

author: nflaig
content: that's what I suggested back then https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/461, devs didn't like it

author: tersec
content: ok, I don't deeply have preferences as to how to do this but "It might be expensive to get them from the bid." which is enough for me for them to be gone from the SSE

author: tersec
content: I'm fine with 583, I'm fine with other approaches too

author: nflaig
content: from implementer side is seems to be the preference to remove kzg commitments, also align more with spec type

author: nflaig
content: mostly asking consumers here if they use them for something but post gloas it's different anyways

author: pk910
content: xatu very likely uses that event,  but I don't think any other tool from our side does.

author: nflaig
content: do you use `kzg_commitments`?

author: nflaig
content: or better question, even if you do, would you still want them after gloas

author: nflaig
content: my intiuition is that anyone who uses these events it purely interested in timing information, not the actual data of the event

author: samcm
content: yeah, it's mostly timing data

author: samcm
content: we use it to determine blob count but we can derive that elsewhere, definitely not a blocker 🙂

author: samcm
content: tyvm for checking
